description: Canonical SSSS Operation Contract conformance fixtures. These define the canonical request/response shapes for the Operation Contract (spec §6) and are the shared test contract every SSSS implementation MUST pass. A host is conformant only if, for every fixture, it produces the expected response and status code (spec §12).

operation.description: Full atomic file write. Creates or fully replaces a VFS file.

patch.description: Partial frontmatter or body merge into an existing VFS file.

event.description: Append-only immutable event. Never overwrites; stored in the SSSS event log.
event.note: Content must be a valid JSON string for the event payload. Gated by the fixed 'write:event' permission (spec §6.3 Stage 5.5), not a resolved document type.

idempotency.behavior: If the same idempotency_key + workspace_id + request_hash is seen within TTL and previously committed=true, the server returns a replay response with the original operation_id without re-applying the mutation. Reusing the same idempotency_key + workspace_id with a different request_hash is an idempotency_conflict.

envelope: type must be one of: operation, patch, event, delete
idempotency_key must be a safe replay identifier (letters, numbers, dot, underscore, hyphen; 1-128 chars)
path must be a relative VFS path string (no leading /)
workspace_id must be a safe filesystem identifier (letters, numbers, dot, underscore, hyphen; 1-128 chars)
content is required for type:operation and type:event
ssss_content: Files in SSSS directories must have YAML frontmatter
Frontmatter must include a 'type' field matching a known primitive
Required fields for each primitive type must be present and non-empty
required_when-conditional fields must be present when their condition matches (spec §9)
Fields with a registry-declared enum must take one of the declared values
See /api/v1/ssss/schema for the full type schema definitions
authorization: Stage 5.5 (Granular Authorization): actor.role='system' bypasses all checks; actor.role='admin' gets write:*/read:*; any other role is resolved from roles/<role>/ROLE.md's permissions list.

A MISSING actor.role is DENIED, not promoted to admin (fail-closed — spec §6.3 Stage 3 MUST: a host exposing the Operation Contract to untrusted clients MUST overwrite actor with verified identity before this stage runs).
event envelopes are gated on the fixed permission 'write:event' (they have no resolved document type).

runtime_contract.description: Workflow runtime conformance metadata (spec §11.8). A daemon or scheduler bridge reads workflow triggers from the vault and derives Operation Contract envelopes; no scheduler database is source-of-truth.

fixtures.name: Create assistant — valid operation
fixtures.description: A well-formed operation to create an assistant definition.

request.content:

type: assistant
title: "Test Bot"
timestamp: "2026-07-02T00:00:00Z"
name: "Test Bot"
description: "A conformance test assistant."
model: "openai/gpt-4o"
---

## Instructions

You are a conformance test assistant.

errors: Missing required field 'name' for type 'assistant' in assistants/bad-bot/ASSISTANT.md.

    concurrency: skip_if_running

1. Gather unread inbox messages.
2. Summarize via LLM.
3. Send digest email.

fixtures.name: Stage 5.5 fail-closed — missing actor is denied, not admin-promoted

request.content:

description: "No Actor Bot SSSS conformance fixture."
timestamp: "2026-07-02T00:00:00Z"
name: "No Actor Bot"

Should never commit without a verified actor.
request.intent: Conformance fixture: envelope with no actor must be denied

field_errors.issue: Missing actor.role — the envelope was not authorized by a verified identity.

fixtures.name: Memory schema_version 2 — all required_when fields present

description: "Prefer Atomic Writes SSSS conformance fixture."

category: patterns
title: "Prefer atomic writes"
status: active
schema_version: 2
confidence: 0.9
importance: 4
modality: should
subject: engine
predicate: performs
object: atomic_write
sentiment_polarity: descriptive
---

Write-then-rename, never write-in-place.
request.intent: Conformance fixture: schema_version 2 memory with all conditional fields

fixtures.name: Memory schema_version 2 — missing required_when fields is denied

fixtures.name: Create viewer security role — read:* only

request.content:
---
type: security_role
title: "Viewer"
description: "Viewer SSSS conformance fixture."
timestamp: "2026-07-02T00:00:00Z"
name: "viewer"
permissions:
  - "read:*"
---

Read-only access; may not write anything, including events.
request.intent: Conformance fixture: create a read-only role

fixtures.name: Event write by viewer role — denied
fixtures.description: The viewer role (read:* only) must be denied on an event write against the fixed 'write:event' permission.

request.content: {"event_type":"should_not_land"}
request.intent: Conformance fixture: event write by a role lacking write:event

fixtures.name: Skill missing the type discriminator is denied

fixtures.name: Skill missing description is denied
fixtures.description: A skill manifest declaring type: skill and name but no description is denied — description is a required_field on the skill primitive (spec §5.4).

error_codes.400: invalid_request — Invalid parameters, directory traversal, malformed request envelope, or path template mismatch
error_codes.401: unauthorized — Missing or invalid auth token / session
error_codes.403: forbidden — Access denied (workspace membership check failed or system scope isolation violation)
error_codes.404: not_found — The requested file to patch or delete does not exist
error_codes.409: lease_conflict, version_conflict, or idempotency_conflict — Lease locked, out-of-date expected version, or idempotency key replay payload mismatch
error_codes.422: validation_failed — Primitive registry/conformance validation errors (contains validation.errors and repair in body)
error_codes.500: internal_error — Unexpected server error, VFS write failed, or DB transactional RPC error

provenance: Seeded from the festech.live reference implementation, which authored the original shared contract; canonicalized here as the vendor-neutral source of truth.
